name: Hrotti
description: Hrotti, meaning 'Striker' or 'Cutter,' is a legendary sword from Norse mythology, primarily known from the Prose Edda. It is described as one of the three treasures forged by the dwarves Brokkr and Eitri for Odin, alongside Gullinbursti (the golden boar) and Draupnir (Odin's ring). Unlike some other famous Norse swords like Gram or Tyrfing, Hrotti's specific powers and feats are not extensively detailed in surviving texts. However, its inclusion among Odin's treasures suggests it was a weapon of exceptional quality, sharpness, and perhaps even magical properties. The sword's association with Odin implies it was a symbol of power, authority, and victory, fitting for the Allfather. Its creation by dwarves further reinforces its status as a superior weapon, as dwarves were renowned for their unparalleled skill in crafting magical items. The lack of specific heroic tales centered around Hrotti, however, leaves its exact nature and role somewhat ambiguous, open to interpretation and speculation.
subtitle: A legendary sword forged by dwarves for Odin.
origin: Forged by the dwarves Brokkr and Eitri as a gift for Odin.

keyMyths.title: The Forging of Odin's Treasures
keyMyths.description: Brokkr and Eitri forged Hrotti along with Gullinbursti and Draupnir as part of a wager with Loki, who believed they couldn't create items superior to the sons of Ivaldi. The resulting treasures, including the mighty Hrotti, proved Loki wrong and secured the dwarves' victory. This highlights the dwarven skill in crafting powerful artifacts.

keyMyths.title: Odin's Armory
keyMyths.description: While no specific battles are attributed to Hrotti, the sword's inclusion among Odin's possessions suggests it was part of his arsenal of powerful weapons and tools. Odin, the god of war, would undoubtedly have possessed many formidable weapons, and Hrotti would have been among the most prized of them.

keyMyths.title: The Symbolism of Dwarven Craftsmanship
keyMyths.description: The fact that Hrotti was forged by dwarves is significant. Dwarves were considered masters of metalworking and magic, and their creations were often imbued with special properties. Hrotti, therefore, represents the pinnacle of dwarven skill and the power that can be harnessed through expert craftsmanship.
keyMyths.source: John Lindow, Norse Mythology: A Guide to the Gods, Heroes, Rituals, and Beliefs

sources.author: Snorri Sturluson
sources.description: The primary source mentioning Hrotti, specifically in the Skáldskaparmál section, detailing the circumstances of its creation.
